LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF RICHARD BOWLING MOTION TO ENFORCE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT CONTINUED FROM MAY-21-2010 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JUN-25-2010, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 302. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR JUDGMENT IS GRANTED AGAINST ADS ONLY. CCP SECTION 664.6 DOES NOT APPLY TO INDIVIDUAL MANAGERS BECAUSE THEY WERE NEVER "PARTIES" TO THIS ACTION. DEFENDANT FAILS TO SHOW THAT IT IS PROPER TO NAME INDIVIDUAL MANAGERS AS JUDGMENT DEBTORS PER CCP SECTION 187. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO PRESENT SUFFICIENT ALTER EGO EVIDENCE. MOTION TO AMEND DENIED BECAUSE MOTION NOT PROPERLY CALENDARED. PLAINTIFF'S REMEDY MAY BE TO COMMENCE A NEW ACTION FOR BREACH OF THE GUARANTEES. THE COURT CONTINUES THE MATTER TO JUNE 25, 2010 FOR COUNSEL TO MEET AND CONFER REGARDING THE AMOUNT OF JUDGMENT. COUNSEL ARE TO SUBMIT A JOINT STATEMENT TO THE COURT BY 6/18/2010, IF THEY CANNOT AGREE ON A JUDGMENT AMOUNT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Jun-18-2010 Continued To Sep-21-10 At 10:00 A.M. In Dept 212
MINI MINUTES: Appearance for Plaintiff. The OSC is re-set to 9/21/2010 at 10:00 AM, in Department 212. Proceedings not reported. (212)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enforce Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar May-21-2010 Continued To Jun-25-10 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF RICHARD BOWLING Motion to Enforce Settlement Agreement. Appearances: Brian D. Henri, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 820-0400 appearing for Plaintiff and Moving party; Vicki Van Fleet, Esq. appearing for Defendant ADS and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Motion granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff's request for judgment is granted against ADS only. CCP section 664.6 does not apply to individual managers because they were never "parties" to this action. Defendant fails to show that it is proper to name individual managers as judgment debtors per CCP section 187. Plaintiff fails to present sufficient alter ego evidence. Motion to amend denied because motion not properly calendared. Plaintiff's remedy may be to commence a new action for breach of the guarantees. The Court continues the matter to June 25, 2010 for counsel to meet and confer regarding the amount of judgment. Counsel are to submit a joint statement to the Court by 6/18/2010, if they cannot agree on a judgment amount. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
